operation: Start on the XY workplane, draw a 6-sided regular polygon with 25mm circumscribed diameter, then extrude it 8mm to form a hexagonal base. Next, create a workplane on the top Z face of this base, then generate an uncombined 12mm tall cylinder with 8mm radius. Define a new workplane on the cylinder's top Z face, offset 15mm along the X axis, 0mm along Y, and -5mm along Z, then add a countersunk hole with 6mm inner diameter, 10mm countersink diameter, 10mm depth, and 4 degree countersink angle. Combine all solid bodies into one part, then apply a 2mm fillet to all edges.
geometry: The model features a short regular hexagonal prism base with a smaller coaxial right cylinder mounted centrally on its top face. A countersunk hole is positioned offset radially outward from the central axis of the cylinder, near the outer edge of the part. All sharp edges, both exterior and around the hole, are rounded with a small uniform fillet.
function: This part is designed as an ergonomic control knob or hexagonal interface adapter, with an integrated countersunk hole for secure fastening to a shaft or fastener, and rounded edges for comfortable, safe handling during operation.
